title: Vitamins, Minerals & Water – Page 423
section: Biology: Vitamins, Minerals, Water & Balanced Diet

questions.question: What is the chemical name of Vitamin D?
questions.explanation: Page 423: Vitamin D chemical name is Calciferol.

questions.question: Deficiency of Vitamin D in children causes:
questions.explanation: Page 423: Vitamin D deficiency causes Rickets in children and Osteomalacia/Osteoporosis in adults.

questions.question: What is the chemical name of Vitamin E?
questions.explanation: Page 423: Vitamin E chemical name is Tocopherol.

questions.question: Deficiency of Vitamin E causes:

options.B: Less fertility and destruction of RBC

questions.explanation: Page 423: Vitamin E deficiency causes less fertility and destruction of RBC.

questions.question: Sources of Vitamin E include:

options.B: Leafy vegetables, Milk, Butter, Sprouted wheat, Vegetable oil

questions.explanation: Page 423: Vitamin E sources include Leafy vegetables, Milk, Butter, Sprouted wheat, Vegetable oil, Wheat germ oil.

questions.question: What is the chemical name of Vitamin K?
questions.explanation: Page 423: Vitamin K chemical name is Phylloquinone.

questions.question: Deficiency of Vitamin K causes:
questions.explanation: Page 423: Vitamin K deficiency causes non-clotting of blood.

questions.question: Cobalt is found in which vitamin?
questions.explanation: Page 423: Cobalt is found in Vitamin B12.

questions.question: Synthesis of Vitamin D takes place through which substance in sunlight?
questions.explanation: Page 423: Synthesis of Vitamin D takes place through cholesterol (ergosterol) present in skin via ultraviolet rays of sunlight.

questions.question: Vitamin D2 is also known as:
questions.explanation: Page 423: Vitamin D2 is known as ergocalciferol.

questions.question: Vitamin K is synthesized in our body by:
questions.explanation: Page 423: Vitamin K is synthesized in our colon by bacteria and from there it is absorbed.

questions.question: Which vitamin is mainly stored in the liver?
questions.explanation: Page 423: Vitamin A is mainly stored in liver.

questions.question: Use of polished rice in human diet causes:
questions.explanation: Page 423: Use of polished rice in human diet causes Beriberi.

questions.question: Which is the richest source of Vitamin C?
questions.explanation: Page 423: Amla is the richest source of Vitamin C.

questions.question: Bleeding gum, falling of teeth, fragile bones, and delayed wound healing are symptoms of deficiency of:
questions.explanation: Page 423: These symptoms occur due to deficiency of Vitamin C.

questions.question: Deficiency of calcium mainly occurs in the absence of which vitamin?
questions.explanation: Page 423: Deficiency of calcium mainly occurs in absence of Vitamin D.

questions.question: The daily requirement of Sodium (as sodium chloride) is:
questions.explanation: Page 423: Daily requirement of Sodium is 2–5 grams (as sodium chloride).

questions.question: Sodium is normally found in which part of the cell?
questions.explanation: Page 423: Sodium is normally found in external fluid of cell.

questions.question: The daily requirement of Potassium is:
questions.explanation: Page 423: Daily requirement of Potassium is approximately 1 gram.

questions.question: Potassium is normally found in:
questions.explanation: Page 423: Potassium is normally found in protoplasm. It is important for different chemical reactions in cells.

questions.question: The daily requirement of Calcium is approximately:
questions.explanation: Page 423: Daily requirement of Calcium is approximately 1.2 grams.

questions.question: Which mineral provides strength to bones and teeth, and helps in blood clotting?
questions.explanation: Page 423: Calcium provides strength to bones and teeth with vitamin, important role in blood formation, muscular contraction, and help in clotting of blood.

questions.question: The daily requirement of Iron for boys and girls respectively is:
questions.explanation: Page 423: Iron daily requirement is 25 mg for boys and 35 mg for girls.

questions.question: Iron is an important component of:
questions.explanation: Page 423: Iron is important component of Red Blood Corpuscles and haemoglobin.

questions.question: Iodine is important for the synthesis of which hormone?

options.C: Thyroxin hormone secreted by Thyroid gland

questions.explanation: Page 423: Iodine is important for synthesis of thyroxin hormone secreted by Thyroid gland.

questions.question: The daily requirement of Iodine is:
questions.explanation: Page 423: Daily requirement of Iodine is 20 mg.

questions.question: Which mineral is required for insulin functioning?
questions.explanation: Page 423: Zinc is required for insulin functioning. Sources include liver and fishes.

questions.question: Magnesium is important for functioning of:
questions.explanation: Page 423: Magnesium is for functioning of muscular system and nervous system.

questions.question: Copper is needed for:

options.C: Formation of haemoglobin and bones, and as a conductor of electron

questions.explanation: Page 423: Copper is needed for formation of haemoglobin and bones and as a conductor of electron.

questions.question: Cobalt is essential for synthesis of:
questions.explanation: Page 423: Cobalt is essential for synthesis of RBC and Vitamin B12.

questions.question: Deficiency of fluoride leads to:
questions.explanation: Page 423: Deficiency of fluoride leads to mottling of teeth.

questions.question: Presence of excess fluorine in water causes:
questions.explanation: Page 423: Presence of excess fluorine in water causes fluorosis.

questions.question: To reduce tooth decay, most toothpastes contain:
questions.explanation: Page 423: To reduce tooth decay most tooth pastes contain fluoride.

questions.question: The hard enamel layer of teeth is made of:
questions.explanation: Page 423: Hard enamel layer of teeth is calcium hydroxy apatite.

questions.question: Deficiency of calcium and iron is generally found in:
questions.explanation: Page 423: Deficiency of calcium and iron is generally found in pregnant women.

questions.question: A typical adult human body contains about how much magnesium?
questions.explanation: Page 423: A typical adult human body contains about 25 grams of magnesium.

questions.question: Water is what percentage of our body weight?
questions.explanation: Page 423: Water is the important component of our body, 60% weight of the body is water.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a main function of water in the body?

options.A: Controls temperature by sweating and vapourization
options.B: Important for excretion of excretory substances

questions.explanation: Page 423: Water functions include temperature control, excretion, and hydrolysis. Vitamin D synthesis is by sunlight/cholesterol, not water.

questions.question: Minerals are:

options.B: Homogenous inorganic material needed for metabolism

questions.explanation: Page 423: Mineral is a homogenous inorganic material needed for body which control the metabolism of body.

questions.question: Sources of Iodine include:

options.B: Sea fish, sea food, green leafy vegetables, iodized salt

questions.explanation: Page 423: Iodine sources include sea fish, sea food, green leafy vegetables, iodized salt.

questions.question: Which mineral deficiency affects the activity of nitrogenase, nitrate reductase, and chlorate reductase in plants?
questions.explanation: Page 423: Deficiency of molybdenum affects the activity of nitrogenase, nitrate reductase and chlorate reductase in plants.

questions.question: Sodium is related to which function in the body?

options.B: Contraction of muscles and transmission of nerve impulses

questions.explanation: Page 423: Sodium is related to contraction of muscles and transmission of nerve impulses in nerve fiber.

questions.question: Main sources of Calcium include:
questions.explanation: Page 423: Calcium sources include milk, cheese, eggs, grains, gram, fish etc.

questions.question: Phosphorus daily requirement is:
questions.explanation: Page 423: Phosphorus daily requirement is 1.2 gram. Sources include milk, cheese, bajra, green leafy vegetables.

questions.question: Phosphorus provides strength to bones and teeth in coordination with:
questions.explanation: Page 423: Phosphorus provides strength to bones and teeth, in coordination with calcium.

questions.question: Synthesis of vitamins can/cannot be done by cells of the body:

options.B: Cannot be done; fulfilled by vitamin-containing food

questions.explanation: Page 423: Synthesis of vitamins cannot be done by the cells of body. It is fulfilled by vitamin containing food.

questions.question: Which vitamins can be synthesized in the human body?
questions.explanation: Page 423: Synthesis of Vitamin D and K takes place in body (D via sunlight, K via colon bacteria).

questions.question: Sources of Vitamin K include:

options.B: Tomato, Soybean oil, Green vegetables, Alfalfa

questions.explanation: Page 423: Vitamin K sources include Tomato, Soybean oil, Green vegetables, Alfalfa etc.

questions.question: Sources of Iron include:

options.C: Albumen of egg, bread, Bajra, Banana, Spinach, Apple

questions.explanation: Page 423: Iron sources include albumen of egg, bread, Bajra, Banana, Spinach apple.

questions.question: Balance Diet is defined as:

options.B: Nutrition in which all important nutrients are available in sufficient quantity

questions.explanation: Page 423: Balance Diet is that nutrition in which all the important nutrients for organism are available in sufficient quantity.
